data.title: Jefferies Q1 revenue beats, helped by robust investment banking activity
data.description:
Jefferies Financial Group (JEF) fiscal Q1 earnings continued to reflect write-offs from some of its investments as it winds down its merchant banking portfolio. Meanwhile, Q1 revenue topped the Wall Street consensus as investment banking revenue benefited from a surge in equity underwriting and strength in advisory revenue.

The company also said its board increased its share repurchase authorization back to a total of $250M after it bought back $174M of shares in its fiscal Q1.

Jefferies (JEF) stock rose 0.6% in after-hours trading on Wednesday.

Q1 EPS from continuing operations of $0.70, lagging the $0.91 consensus, increased from $0.57 in last year’s Q1. During the most recent quarter, the company took a $36M non-cash writedown of goodwill associated with the announced sale of Tessellis and $17M of losses related to Market Financial Solutions and First Brands.

Jefferies (JEF) said its direct exposure to First Brands, the auto parts firm that collapsed last September, is now zero.

"We made progress in the further wind-down of our legacy merchant banking portfolio, with the announced sale of Tessellis," said CEO Richard Handler and President Brian Friedman. "We expect this transaction to close in the first quarter of 2027. Going forward, our financial results will increasingly reflect our core business activities."

Revenue for the quarter ended Jan. 31, 2026, was $2.02B, vs. the $1.98B consensus, up 27% from $1.59B in the year-ago period.

Investment banking net revenue rose to $1.02B from $701M in Q1 2025, with equity underwriting increasing 138% to $306M and debt underwriting declining 8.8% to $182M. Advisory net revenue of $527M jumped 33% Y/Y on increased deal volumes across several sectors.

Total capital markets revenue of $779M logged a 12% Y/Y increase.

Asset management fees and investment return revenues were $159M, up 91% compared with the prior year quarter.

Q1 total noninterest expense of $1.80B vs. $1.44B a year ago.

The compensation ratio of 53.8% increased from 52.2% in Q4 2025 and 52.8% in Q1 2025.

Return on adjusted tangible shareholders equity improved to 10.9% from 8.0%.

data.title: Stocks to watch after market on Wednesday: DRVN, EGO, FBLG
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages closed higher on Wednesday as the U.S. reportedly sent Iran a 15-point plan to end the war in the Middle East.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished higher by +0.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended up +0.5%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) pushed higher by +0.7%.

Here are the three stocks to watch after market:

Driven Brands (DRVN) rose 1.6% in after-hours trading after activist investor Adam Wyden’s hedge fund ADW Capital, which holds a 2.5% stake, pushed the automotive service provider to explore a sale or breakup. In a letter to the board, Wyden called for an immediate strategic review and criticized private-equity owner Roark Capital, which holds a 60% stake, for mismanaging the business while being distracted by its food brands including Dunkin’ and Arby’s. 

Eldorado Gold (EGO) is in focus after proxy advisory firm Institutional Shareholder Services recommended shareholders vote to approve the issuance of shares in connection with the company’s proposed acquisition of Foran Mining (FMCXF). The deal values the Canadian copper-focused developer at approximately C$3.8 billion.

FibroBiologics (FBLG) announced its board approved a 1-for-20 reverse stock split to regain compliance with Nasdaq Capital Market minimum bid price requirements. The split takes effect March 30, reducing outstanding shares to approximately 3.5 million from roughly 70.3 million.

data.title: Hallador Energy locks in power capacity pricing at double current levels
data.description:
Hallador Energy (HNRG) up 2.1% post-market Wednesday after saying it signed a three-year agreement to sell substantially all of its remaining accredited capacity to an unnamed utility customer for 2026-28 at record pricing for the company.

Hallador (HNRG) said the capacity was priced at ~2x capacity pricing levels currently embedded in its forward sales book and is expected to generate ~$86M in total revenues over the three-year term.

Assuming the increased pricing levels, the company said its capacity revenues have the potential to increase to ~$130M annually starting in 2029, which would be in addition to energy revenue.

"This transaction establishes a step change for higher capacity pricing at our Merom facility and reflects the strength of demand we are seeing in the market. It also provides a strong foundation as we negotiate additional long-term capacity agreements," Hallador (HNRG) Chairman and CEO Brent Bilsland said.

data.title: Pizza Pizza Royalty reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Pizza Pizza Royalty press release (PZRIF): Q4
  • Adjusted earnings per share was flat at C$0.245
  • Total system sales of C$164M.
  • Same store sales increased 0.2%
  • Royalty Pool sales increased 2.2%

data.title: Activist investor pushes Driven Brands to explore a sale - WSJ
data.description:
An activist investor is pushing automotive service provider Driven Brands (DRVN) to explore a sale or breakup. Driven rose 1.6% in after-hours trading.

Adam Wyden's hedge fund ADW Capital, which has a 2.5% stake in Driven Brands (DRVN), wants the company to immediately start a strategic review process, according to a WSJ report on Wednesday, which cited a letter sent to Driven's board. Wyden also criticized Driven Brand's private-equity owner Roark Capital of mismanaging its business.

Wyden wrote in the letter that Driven's (DRVN)  individual companies, including Meineke and Auto Glass Now, should either be broken up or the whole company should be sold to a competitor or another PE firm, according to the report. If Driven were to do such a move, its shares may be worth more than $30 a share. 

Wyden also wrote that Roark, which has a 60% stake in Driven (DRVN) after taking it public in 2021, has been distracted by its food brands such as Dunkin' and Arby's, the WSJ said. 

The sale push comes after Driven's (DRVN) shares plunged 30% on Feb. 25 after the company reported "material errors" in its financial statement in recent years and delayed the release of its Q4 results.

data.title: Apollo prices $750 million senior notes offering
data.description:
• Apollo Global Management (APO) has priced an offering of $750 million aggregate principal amount of its 5.700% senior notes due 2036.
  • The notes will bear interest at 5.700% per annum, payable semi-annually in arrears on March 30 and September 30 of each year, commencing on September 30, 2026.
  • The offering is expected to close on March 30, 2026.
  • APO -0.0% after hours to $109.8
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: EJH announces 1-for-25 share consolidation to meet Nasdaq rule
data.description:
• E-Home Household Service (EJH) will implement a 1-for-25 share consolidation effective March 30, 2026.
  • Outstanding shares will decrease from ~80.0M pre-split to ~3.2M post-split.
  • The move is aimed at complying with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement.

data.title: FibroBiologics to implement 1-for-20 reverse stock split to meet Nasdaq requirement
data.description:
• FibroBiologics (FBLG) said on Wednesday its board approved a 1-for-20 reverse stock split to regain compliance with minimum bid price requirements for listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • The reverse split will take effect on March 30, with trading on a split-adjusted basis beginning the same day under the ticker “FBLG”.
  • The company said every 20 shares will be consolidated into one, reducing outstanding shares to about 3.5 million from roughly 70.3 million, subject to adjustments for fractional shares.

  • FibroBiologics said the move, previously authorized by shareholders, is intended to restore compliance and potentially broaden investor interest.

  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Zhihu outlines dual-track AI-driven growth strategy as 2026 begins
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Zhihu Inc. (ZH) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Yuan Zhou, represented by AI agent Victor Zhou, highlighted Zhihu's "first ever full year non-GAAP profit," with adjusted net income reaching RMB 37.9 million and marking a turnaround from the prior year's loss. Zhou emphasized, "This historic milestone validates our strategic transformation and underscores the structural durability of our operational leverage."
  • The company reported significant growth in user engagement, stating, "In Q4, average daily time spent per user increased to over 41 minutes on the platform." Professional AI-related content increased by over 30% year-over-year, and high-quality content creation surged more than 20% year-over-year in Q4.
  • Zhihu accelerated AI integration, focusing on "rapidly building industry-leading export data solutions and deploying AI productivity tools to accelerate IP monetization of our Yan'an Stories franchise."
  • The company noted a shift in commercialization strategy, with "total revenue trend improved meaningfully in the fourth quarter, driven by a double-digit sequential increase in marketing services." Marketing services revenue reached RMB 234.8 million, up 24% sequentially.
  • CFO Wang Han stated, "2025 represents a structural upgrade in Zhihu's financial profile... driven by sustained cost discipline, improved operating leverage and tighter expense control, while maintaining healthy gross margins."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated the focus on leveraging AI for commercialization and maintaining operational discipline. CFO Wang Han indicated that the company is "not pursuing a single path of delivering profitability this year, more profitability next year," but instead will "stay focused on the opportunities created by AI and invest behind them."
  • The priority for 2026 includes "continuing to use AI to improve efficiency and deliver a better product experience for our users and the content creators" while developing new AI businesses, particularly "AI-enabled short-form drama and comic adaption" and "AI data services."
  • The company aims "to deliver growth in new AI-driven revenue stream... and to keep the overall bottom line on a healthy and responsive track."

Financial Results

  • Total revenues for the quarter were RMB 643.5 million. The company highlighted a "clear top line recovery trajectory" as the pace of sequential decline narrowed.
  • Marketing services revenue was RMB 234.8 million, with a 24% sequential increase, and paid membership revenue was RMB 333.5 million. Average monthly paid members reached 12.2 million.
  • Gross profit for the quarter was RMB 344.8 million, and gross margin came in at 53.6%.
  • Operating expenses totaled RMB 608.7 million, with a one-time non-cash goodwill impairment charge of RMB 126.3 million. Selling and marketing expenses decreased 13% year-over-year, and R&D expenses decreased 16% year-over-year.
  • GAAP net loss for the quarter was RMB 210.8 million, while non-GAAP adjusted net loss was RMB 39.4 million.
  • The company reported RMB 4.5 billion in cash and equivalents as of December 31, 2025, and repurchased 31.1 million Class A shares for USD 66.5 million during the year.

Q&A

  • Xueqing Zhang, China International Capital Corporation: Asked about 2026 earnings outlook and balancing investment with cash flow and profitability. CFO Wang Han responded that while 2025 showed profitability, "we are not pursuing a single path of delivering profitability this year, more profitability next year... we want to deliver growth in new AI-driven revenue stream... and to keep the overall bottom line on a healthy and responsive track."
  • Kewei Chen, Haitong International: Inquired about commercialization strategy and 2026 priorities. CFO Wang Han explained the main focus will be "using AI to improve efficiency and deliver a better product experience," and developing "AI-enabled short-form drama and comic adaption" as well as "AI data services" as new revenue streams, stressing discipline in investment and focus on positive cash flow initiatives.
  • Yi Jing Wei, Citigroup: Asked about the impact of AI on the community and upcoming product upgrades. CEO Zhou Yuan described increased AI adoption, sharing that "in 4Q, both the coverage and frequency of the positive user interactions on the platform increased year-over-year," and highlighted new AI features like the AI reading panel on PC, which "meaningfully improved the reading efficiency of long-form content."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised forward-looking questions focused on profitability, commercialization strategy, and tangible impacts of AI integration, indicating a neutral to slightly positive tone with a focus on sustainable growth and cash flow discipline.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone, emphasizing operational resilience, strong execution, and selective investment in AI-driven initiatives. Phrases such as "we are confident," "we believe," and "we want to deliver growth in new AI-driven revenue stream" reflected continued optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone signals a shift from cautious optimism about reaching profitability to a more assertive approach toward AI-driven expansion and monetization.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The previous quarter focused on reaching non-GAAP profitability and navigating the bottoming out of marketing services, while this quarter celebrated the achievement of full-year non-GAAP profit and emphasized a "clear top line recovery trajectory."
  • Guidance language shifted from achieving breakeven to scaling AI-driven commercialization and exploring new AI business models.
  • Analyst questions moved from AI adoption and ecosystem health to monetization strategies and the balance between investment and profitability.
  • Management's tone evolved from cautious and process-oriented to more confident and strategic, highlighting discipline and focus on scalable AI revenue streams.
  • Key metrics such as user engagement, high-quality content growth, and paid memberships continued to rise, though paid membership numbers declined sequentially.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited a one-time non-cash goodwill impairment charge of RMB 126.3 million related to prior acquisitions, reflecting market-driven lower valuations.
  • CFO Wang Han stressed the company "will be disciplined in selecting new initiatives concentrating our investments on areas with visible ROI potential and a strong fit with Zhihu's core strength."
  • Analyst concerns centered on maintaining profitability while investing in new AI businesses, with management reiterating a commitment to operational discipline and not reverting "to the old model of burning significant cash for growth."

Final Takeaway

Zhihu's management underscored the milestone of achieving its first full year of non-GAAP profitability in 2025, positioning the company to accelerate AI-driven commercialization and explore new scalable growth engines. The dual-track strategy for 2026 focuses on enhancing core community efficiency with AI and developing new AI-powered revenue streams in content adaptation and data services, all while maintaining disciplined investment and a strong commitment to operational resilience and long-term value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: 3 things to look out for on Thursday
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes moved higher on Wednesday amid reports that the U.S. had delivered a 15-point proposal to Iran aimed at ending the Middle East conflict.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) ended higher by +0.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) added +0.5%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) pushed up by +0.7%.

Now, here are three focus points for investors on Thursday:

  • Traders will be closely watching Thursday’s initial and continuing jobless claims data ahead of the open, as investors look for fresh signals on the labor market. The weekly readings will be scrutinized for any signs of cooling employment trends or rising layoffs that could influence expectations for economic growth and Federal Reserve policy.
  • Market participants are expected to keep a close eye on oil prices, with crude holding near the $90-per-barrel level, as U.S.-Iran tensions continue to heighten uncertainty. Ongoing concerns around disruptions to key shipping routes and energy output are adding to volatility across global energy markets.
  • Investors will also be monitoring any new developments surrounding potential peace talks between the U.S. and Iran, as the ongoing Middle East conflict continues to rattle markets. While reports suggest indirect or “backchannel” discussions may have taken place, uncertainty remains high, with mixed signals on whether a concrete framework for negotiations will emerge.
